PH: Recruiter sees Empty state of email task if opened
issues.description:
h2. User Story

*As a* Recruiter,
*I want* to see a clear empty state when I open the 2-way email task in the sliding side panel before any thread exists,
*So that* I understand what to do next and can easily initiate a new conversation for this job application.
h2. Scenarios
h3. Scenario 1: Opening Email task initially shows the empty state for new conversations

*Given* I am a recruiter with access to the 2-way email task and no conversation exists yet for this job application
*When* I open the email task in the sliding side panel
*Then* I see the designated empty state UI for "no messages yet" and accompanying graphic
*And* I see the Compose Email button and the New button, and the Grow button, and header text "Conversational Email" - check with [~johnpatrick.mugumya] 

 

*Dev Notes:*
 * UI + small piece of XO
 * XO changes will include:
 ** New labels for the empty state text - {color:#ff8b00}should there be different text for an agency and non-agency candidate?{color}, "compose email" button text, "Expand Panel", "Collapse Panel" button text
 ** Adding those new labels and New, 6104$8743 to the UI Label Container Messaging Layout, 11111$220 (this let's us easily use them in the UI code)
 ** Adding the new domain onto the endpoint layouts/conversationContextInstance/view (GET) (v1 -  ), 5557$42319
 * UI changes:
 ** Update Header and EmptyState components to conditionally display Email specific UI
 *** For Header, this should include the style of header from the figma with the separated title and subtitle and 2 buttons - for now the buttons do not do anything
 *** For EmptyState, this should include the new text as per figma and the compose email button as well as the existing empty state graphic

issues.summary: PH: Recruiter receives a notification
issues.description:
*As a* Recruiter,
*I want to* be notified in Workday when a candidate or agency user replies to my 2-way email,
*So that* I can respond promptly within the system without needing to monitor an external inbox.
h2. Scenarios
h3. Scenario 1: Inbound reply triggers framework notification for participants

*Given* an inbound 2-way email reply is successfully ingested and matched to an active thread
*When* the system processes the message
*Then* a notification is automatically triggered for the primary recruiter and any other recruiter who has participated in that conversation
*And* the notification appears in the "My Conversations" menu (falling back to the standard "Bell" icon if the recruiter lacks specific domain access).
h3. Scenario 2: Clicking the notification opens the conversation context

*Given* I have received a UI notification for a new candidate or agency reply
*When* I click the notification
*Then* I am navigated directly to the corresponding candidate profile
*And* the sliding side panel automatically opens to display the new message within the correct 2-way email thread.
h3. Scenario 3: Optional delivery of standard email alerts

*Given* the tenant administrator has configured routing rules to send email notifications for "Candidate SMS/Email Messages"
*When* a new inbound reply is processed
*Then* I additionally receive a standard Workday email notification alerting me to the new message in the system.

issues.summary: PH: Admin can enable or disable 2-way email as a comms channel
issues.description:
h2. User story

*As a* tenant admin
*I want* to enable or disable 2-way email as a comms channel for my tenant
*So that* I control rollout and policy alignment before recruiters see compose surfaces

h2. Scenarios

h3. Scenario 1: Enable channel for pilot tenant

*Given* I am a tenant admin
*When* I enable 2-way email as a comms channel
*Then* recruiters in scope see the email affordances per configuration

h3. Scenario 2: Disable mid-flight blocks new sends

*Given* 2-way email was enabled and a recruiter has an unsent draft open
*When* I disable the channel
*Then* the recruiter cannot successfully send new messages from the governed surface and they see messaging consistent with disabled channel policy

h3. Scenario 3: Audit entry for toggle change

*Given* I change the channel toggle
*When* the change is saved
*Then* an auditable record exists suitable for enterprise compliance review

issues.summary: PH: Recruiter opens the message to read and reply
issues.description:
*As a* Recruiter,
*I want to* expand the sliding side panel to a wide view to read the full content of an email and initiate my reply,
*So that* I can comfortably manage and complete the communication loop without leaving Workday.
h2. Scenarios
h3. Scenario 1: Expanding to "Wide View" to read full content

*Given* an inbound candidate or agency reply exists in the basic thread
*When* I click the specific message OR click the "Grow/Expand" button on the panel
*Then* the sliding side panel expands to the wide form factor overlaying the main page content
*And* I can read the full message body and metadata with correct attribution in the expanded view.
h3. Scenario 2: Initiating a reply from the Wide View

*Given* I am viewing the full content of a message in the expanded wide view
*When* I click the "Reply" button
*Then* the rich text editor (Compose view) opens within the wide panel.
h3. Scenario 3: Action controls hidden when conversation is disabled

*Given* I am viewing a message in the wide view
*When* the conversation is in a disabled state (e.g., Closed, candidate Opted-Out, or email address changed)
*Then* the "Reply" and "Add/New" buttons are completely hidden from the UI
*And* I see a clear informational message explaining why I can no longer interact with this thread.

issues.summary: PH: Recruiter can compose an email to non-agency candidate
issues.description:
h2. User Story

*As a* Recruiter communicating with a non-agency candidate
*I want to* compose 2-way emails only when the candidate is opted in and I have "Modify/Send" access to the Conversational Email security domain,
*So that* I maintain data compliance and am not shown compose controls I cannot use.
h2. Scenarios
h3. Scenario 1: Compose for authorised, opted-in non-agency candidates

*Given* the non-agency candidate is opted in to receive emails and I have "Modify" access to the Conversational Email security domain
*And* I have opened the 2-way email task in the sliding side panel
*When* I can click the Add (Plus) or Reply buttons (if available) to open the compose view.
*Then* I see the compose view, where I can enter the body, subject, and send to the permitted recipient
h3. Scenario 2: Compose controls are hidden when a non-agency candidate is not opted in

*Given* the non-agency candidate has not opted in (or has opted out) to receive emails 
*When* I open the 2-way email task in the sliding side panel 
*Then* the compose controls (such as the Add/Compose buttons) are completely hidden from the recruiter, as I am not permitted to send a new email.
h3. Scenario 3: Compose controls are hidden for read-only security access

*Given* my security role grants me "View" access but lacks "Modify" access to the Conversational Email domain
*When* I open the 2-way email task in the sliding side panel for a non-agency candidate
*Then* the compose controls (Add and Reply) are hidden based on conditional logic on the UI, allowing me to view historical messages without the ability to author new ones.

*I want* the email composition view to visually render custom dropdowns for the "To" and "From" fields, alongside a standard "Subject" field,

*When* I view the email header
*Then* a custom dropdown component is visually displayed for the "To" field instead of the native Message Builder free-text field
h3. Scenario 2: Custom UI dropdown replaces the native "From" field

*Given* the email composition view is rendered in the sliding side panel
*When* I view the email header
*Then* a custom dropdown component is visually displayed for the "From" field instead of the native Message Builder free-text field
h3. Scenario 3: Subject field is rendered as part of the email header

*Given* the email composition view is rendered in the sliding side panel
*When* I view the email header
*Then* the "Subject" text field is visually rendered alongside the "To" and "From" dropdowns

issues.summary: PH: Recruiter attaches documents (UI only)
issues.description:
h2. User Story

*As a* Recruiter,
*I want* an attachment upload component visually rendered within the email composition view,
*So that* the UI framework is in place to upload documents
h2. Scenarios
h3. Scenario 1: Visual rendering of the attachment component

*Given* the email composition view is open in the sliding side panel
*When* I view the Message Builder area
*Then* an attachment upload button/component is visually rendered and accessible.
h3. Scenario 2: UI file selection and visual attachment state

*Given* I interact with the attachment upload component
*When* I select an allowed file from my local system
*Then* the file visually appears as an attachment pill/item on my email draft
*And* I can visually remove the attachment from the draft using a "remove/X" icon
h3. Scenario 3: Client-side validation for disallowed types/sizes 

*Given* I am attempting to upload a file to the UI component
*When* I select a disallowed file type or a file that exceeds the standard Workday attachment size limits
*Then* I am prevented from attaching the file
*And* I see a specific client-side validation error in the UI.

*I want* the email composition view to visually render the Message Builder rich text editor, its formatting toolbar, and the "Send" and "Discard" buttons,

*Given* the Message Builder formatting toolbar is rendered
*When* I view the available toolbar options
*Then* the GenAI (AI Assistant / purple sparkle) button is explicitly excluded/hidden from the toolbar configuration
h3. Scenario 3: Visual rendering of the Send and Discard buttons (UI Only)

*Given* the email composition view is open in the sliding side panel
*When* I look underneath the rich text editor
*Then* the "Send" and "Discard" buttons are visually displayed at the bottom of the panel
*And* these buttons are rendered for visual layout purposes only.

issues.summary: PH: Recruiter attaches non-supported files
issues.description:
h2. User Story

*As a* Recruiter,
*I want to* be stopped clearly and immediately by the UI when I try to attach non-supported or oversized files,
*So that* I do not accidentally send insecure attachments or exceed system limits, saving me from backend delivery failures.
h2. Scenarios
h3. Scenario 1: Client-side block for disallowed file extensions

*Given* I attempt to select an attachment from my local system
*When* the file is a disallowed or executable type (e.g., .exe, .bat, .js) as defined by the tenant setup
*Then* the UI immediately rejects the file upon selection
*And* I see a clear client-side error message explaining that the file type is not supported
*And* the file is not staged in the email draft.
h3. Scenario 2: Single file exceeds maximum configured size

*Given* I attempt to select an attachment from my local system
*When* the individual file size exceeds the maximum configured attachment size limit
*Then* the UI immediately rejects the file
*And* I see size-specific guidance in the error message (e.g., "File exceeds the maximum limit of X MB").
h3. Scenario 3: Cumulative attachments exceed total email size limit

*Given* I have already successfully staged one or more valid attachments to my email draft
*When* I attempt to add another file that would push the total combined email size (body + all attachments) over the system's maximum payload limit (e.g., 30 MB)
*Then* the UI prevents the newest file from being attached
*And* I see an error message indicating that the total email size limit has been reached.

issues.summary: PH: Recruiter sees UI error validations on Send
issues.description:
h2. User Story

*As a* Recruiter,
*I want* the system to perform inline validation when I click "Send" on an email draft,
*So that* I am prevented from dispatching messages with missing required fields (like To, Subject) and avoid failed backend round-trips.
h2. Scenarios
h3. Scenario 1: Blocked send due to missing Subject

*Given* the "Subject" field is empty
*When* I click the "Send" button
*Then* I see inline/field-level validation "Error: Required field." indicating the subject is required, and the email dispatch is blocked.
h3. Scenario 2: Blocked send due to empty "To" field

*Given* the "To" dropdown field is empty (no recipient is selected)
*When* I click the "Send" button
*Then* I see inline/field-level validation "Error: Required field." indicating a recipient is required, and the email dispatch is blocked.
h3. Scenario 3: Multiple validation errors trigger a blocking alert

*Given* multiple required fields are missing or in an invalid state (e.g., the "To" dropdown is empty AND the "Subject" field is empty)
*When* I click the "Send" button
*Then* the email dispatch is blocked
*And* I see a standard red blocking alert summarising the total number of issues (e.g., "2 Errors"), which guides me to correct all invalid fields before the system will allow a successful round-trip.

issues.summary: PH: Send Message (No Attachments)
issues.description:
h2. User story

*As a* Recruiter
*I want to* send my composed email with no attachments and see the outbound message appear in-thread with its updated delivery state
*So that* I have a trusted, auditable record of my communication history 
h2. Scenarios
h3. Scenario 1: Send and in-thread display (No Attachments)

*Given* a valid composed email state containing no attachments
*When* I click the "Send" button
*Then* the message text payload is successfully dispatched to the backend via REST API
*And* the message immediately appears in the sliding side panel conversation thread with a "Sent" status tag.
h3. Scenario 2: Client polling for delayed visibility

*Given* I have successfully dispatched a message via the REST API
*When* there is a delay in the backend processing making the message visible in the thread return data
*Then* the client UI handles this by polling/retrying the GET methods so the message correctly appears in the thread without requiring a manual page refresh.
h3. Scenario 3: Duplicate send prevention

*Given* I am clicking the "Send" button
*When* the REST API call is in flight
*Then* the client automatically disables the "Send" button to prevent accidental double-taps from creating duplicate logical messages in the backend

h2. Story 1: M5 - Expand Panel to Wide View (Structural / UI Framework)

*As a* Recruiter,
*I want* the sliding side panel to dynamically expand to a wide reading layout when I click "Grow" or open a single message,
*So that* long-form emails are comfortably readable compared to the narrow view used for SMS or Notes.
h2. Scenarios
h3. Scenario 1: Panel expansion on standard screens (Modal & Drop Shadow)

*Given* the sliding side panel is open in the narrow view on a standard screen
*When* an expand event is dispatched (via clicking the "Grow" button or a single message)
*Then* the panel expands to the wide layout
*And* a modal/drop shadow appears on the rest of the page behind the panel to focus user attention.
h3. Scenario 2: "Overlay" behavior on Extra Large (XL) screens

*Given* the panel is open and the main page content is currently "pushed" beside it due to an XL screen resolution
*When* an expand event is dispatched to grow the panel
*Then* the panel expands and safely overlays the main page content rather than attempting to push the content further off-screen.
h3. Scenario 3: Toggling back to the narrow view (Shrink)

*Given* the sliding side panel is currently expanded to the wide view
*When* the user triggers a shrink event by clicking the action again or switching to another channel (like SMS)
*Then* the panel toggles back to its standard narrow width and the background drop shadow is removed.

issues.summary: PH: Recruiter sees error messages if their email has bounced
issues.description:
h2. User Story

*As a* Recruiter,
*I want* clear bounce diagnostics on my affected outbound messages in the sliding side panel,
*So that* I can quickly understand why my email was not delivered and correct any addressable issues
h2. Scenarios
h3. Scenario 1: Generic / Default Not-Delivered (Hard Bounce)

*Given* an outbound email fails to deliver and the provider returns a standard hard bounce event
*When* I view the message in the expanded wide view
*Then* I see the headline: "We couldn't deliver this email."
*And* I see the body: "Try sending it again or contact the recipient using another method."
h3. Scenario 2: Spam / Sender-Reputation Variant (Complaint)

*Given* the failure is classified as a complaint (recipient marked a previous email as spam)
*When* I view the message in the expanded wide view
*Then* I see the headline: "We couldn't deliver this email."
*And* I see the body: "The recipient marked a previous email as spam. Avoid emailing this address to protect your sender reputation, and contact the recipient using another method."
h3. Scenario 3: Unsubscribe / Consent Variant (Subscription)

*Given* the failure is classified as an unsubscribe/opt-out event
*When* I view the message in the expanded wide view
*Then* I see the headline: "We couldn't deliver this email."
*And* I see the body: "The recipient unsubscribed from your emails. You'll need their explicit opt-in before you can email them again. Try contacting them using another method."
h3. Scenario 4: Virus / Unsafe Content Variant (AWS SES: Reject)

*Given* Amazon SES accepted the email but blocked delivery because the content or attachment was flagged as a virus
*When* I view the message in the expanded wide view
*Then* I see the headline: "We couldn't deliver this email."
*And* I see the body: "This email was blocked because it contained content that was flagged as a virus. Remove any attachments and try sending it again."
h3. Scenario 5: Temporary Server Delay Variant (AWS SES: DeliveryDelay)

*Given* the failure is classified as a temporary delay (e.g., recipient's inbox is full or server is unavailable)
*When* I view the message in the expanded wide view
*Then* I see the headline: "We couldn't deliver this email."
*And* I see the body: "The recipient's server is temporarily unavailable. We'll automatically keep trying, but if it's urgent, contact them using another method."
h3. Scenario 6: Technical Issue Variant (Catch-All)

*Given* the failure is classified as a technical issue which did not compirse of any of the variants in scenarios 1-5
*When* I view the message in the expanded wide view
*Then* I see the headline: "We couldn't deliver this email."

issues.summary: PH: Recruiter sees error when sending message (REST error)
issues.description:
h2. User Story

*As a* Recruiter,
*I want* structured UI error handling when my email fails to send due to a REST API error,
*So that* I understand why the send was blocked and what manual actions I need to take to resolve the issue.
h2. Scenarios
h3. Scenario 1: Mid-session state change blocks send (Opt-out or Security Loss)

*Given* I am actively drafting a message in the sliding side panel
*When* I click "Send" but the REST endpoint fails because the underlying system state changed (e.g., the candidate opted out or security access was revoked)
*Then* the send is completely blocked and automated retries are not attempted
*And* I see a clear error message explaining the change in status and instructing me to copy my email message, and then to refresh the page.
h3. Scenario 2: Generic system/network failure during send

*Given* I attempt to send a drafted email
*When* the REST API fails due to a generic server error, timeout, or network issue (e.g., 500-level error)
*Then* the UI safely blocks the send and displays a standard error message indicating the system could not process the request.

issues.summary: PH: Recruiter sees info message and cannot send email if the candidate changes their email
issues.description:
h2. User Story

*As a* Recruiter,
*I want to* be blocked from sending new messages and see a clear explanation if a candidate's primary home email address changes mid-thread,
*So that* I do not accidentally send communications to an outdated or incorrect address, ensuring data privacy and accuracy.
h2. Scenarios
h3. Scenario 1: Candidate email change hides actions and displays an info message

*Given* an active 2-way email conversation exists with a candidate
*When* the candidate's primary home email address changes in the system (e.g., via a profile merge)
*Then* the "Reply" and "Add/New" buttons are completely hidden from the UI
*And* I see a clear informational message in the sliding side panel explaining that the candidate's email has changed and new messages cannot be sent in this thread.
h3. Scenario 2: Historical messages remain accessible as read-only

*Given* a conversation is blocked due to an email address change
*When* I view the thread history in the sliding side panel
*Then* I can still read the full history of prior messages sent under the old email address to preserve the audit trail
*But* the system prevents me from replying or adding new messages to this specific conversation thread.

*I want* my conversation view to be strictly scoped to only the email threads involving myself and the recruiter,

issues.summary: PH: Candidate attaches non-supported files
issues.description:
h2. User Story

*As a* Recruiter,
*I want* inbound emails containing unsupported, oversized, or malicious attachments to be safely flagged in the conversation UI,
*So that* I am aware the candidate attempted to share a file, even if malware or system limits prevented the attachment from being delivered.
h2. Scenarios
h3. Scenario 1: UI displays a system message for blocked/unsupported file types

*Given* a candidate replies with an attachment using a system-blocked file format (e.g., .exe, .bat, .js)
*When* the UI fetches the ingested message payload from the backend
*Then* the UI displays the email body but the attachment is safely stripped
*And* an automated system message or error indicator is surfaced to the recruiter explaining the attachment was rejected.
h3. Scenario 2: UI displays a system message for virus scanner failures

*Given* a candidate sends an email containing an attachment with malware
*When* the backend virus scanning service flags and blocks the file during ingestion
*Then* the UI displays an automated system message in the conversation thread indicating that the candidate's attachment was blocked due to a virus.
h3. Scenario 3: UI surfaces a system message for oversized payload bounces

*Given* a candidate attempts to send an email where the total size exceeds the 30 MB maximum limit
*When* the backend rejects the payload and the email bounces
*Then* the UI displays an automated system message in the thread indicating that the candidate attempted to send a message, but it failed/bounced due to exceeding size limits.

issues.summary: PH: Recruiter can compose an email to agency user or agency candidate
issues.description:
h2. User Story

*As a* Recruiter,
*I want to* access the email compose view on an agency candidate's profile and have the system validate the recipient's opt-in eligibility only at the time of sending,
*So that* I am not blocked from emailing agency users (who do not require opt-in) while still maintaining data compliance when attempting to email candidates.
h2. Scenarios
h3. Scenario 1: Compose controls are always visible upfront for agency candidate profiles

*Given* I am viewing an agency candidate's profile (regardless of the candidate's opt-in status)
*When* I look at the 2-way email task in the sliding side panel
*Then* the compose controls (such as the Add button) are visible and available to use, because the system does not yet know whether I intend to email the candidate or the agency user.
h3. Scenario 2: Successfully sending an email to an Agency User

*Given* I have opened the email compose view on an agency candidate's profile
*When* I select an Agency User as the recipient and click "Send"
*Then* the email is dispatched successfully without any opt-in validation blocking the action, as agency users do not require explicit opt-in.
h3. Scenario 3: Successfully sending an email to an opted-in Agency Candidate

*Given* I have opened the email compose view on an agency candidate's profile
*When* I select the Agency Candidate as the recipient, the candidate has explicitly opted in to receive emails, and I click "Send"
*Then* the send validation passes and the email is dispatched successfully.
h3. Scenario 4: Send validation blocks emails to an opted-out Agency Candidate

*Given* I have opened the email compose view on an agency candidate's profile
*When* I select the Agency Candidate as the recipient, the candidate has not opted in (or has opted out), and I click "Send"
*Then* the send validation fails, the system blocks the email from dispatching, and I am prevented from emailing the non-consenting candidate.

issues.summary: PH: Recruiter can select To address from list of valid email addresses
issues.description:
h2. User Story

*As a* Recruiter,
*I want to* select my "To" address from a dynamically populated list of candidates and agency users, along with their system-registered Home email address,
*So that* I can quickly identify and reply to the appropriate person in their conversation
h2. Scenarios
h3. Scenario 1: Populating the "To" dropdown for standard Non-Agency Candidates

*Given* the email composition view is open for a non-agency candidate profile
*When* I open the "To" dropdown field
*Then* the field is pre-populated with the candidate's Home email address
*And* I can select from a dynamically populated list containing the candidate's available email addresses
*And* the dropdown items explicitly include corresponding visual labels (e.g., "Candidate") to dictate exactly which conversation my reply is directed to and for consistency with scenarios where there are agency users 
h3. Scenario 2: Populating the "To" dropdown for Agency Candidates

*Given* the email composition view is open for an agency candidate profile
*When* I open the "To" dropdown field
*Then* I can select from a dynamically populated list containing both the Candidate's name and email address and the associated Agency User's name and email address
*And* the dropdown items explicitly include corresponding visual labels (e.g., "Candidate" and "Agency") to dictate exactly which conversation my reply is directed to.

issues.summary: PH: Email communications are visible in Activity Stream, Timeline and Candidate Communications
issues.description:
h2. User Story

*As a* Recruiter,
*I want* all 2-way email communications to be automatically logged in the Job Application Activity Stream, the Recruiting History timeline, and the Candidate Communication report,
*So that* there is a complete, auditable history of all candidate and agency interactions accessible to authorized users.
h2. Scenarios
h3. Scenario 1: Recording events in the Job Application Activity Stream

*Given* a 2-way email conversation occurs in the context of a specific job application
*When* I navigate to the Activity Stream tab on that candidate's job application
*Then* the email interaction is automatically recorded and visible in the stream.
h3. Scenario 2: Displaying email events on the Recruiting History Timeline

*Given* a 2-way email has been sent to or received from a candidate or agency user
*When* I view the candidate's profile
*Then* the email event is recorded chronologically on the Recruiting History timeline to maintain an auditable interaction history.
h3. Scenario 3: Logging to the Candidate Communication Report

*Given* an inbound or outbound 2-way email is successfully processed by the system
*When* I run or view the Candidate Communication report
*Then* the email is recorded as a line item
*And* the report accurately displays the message content, sender, recipient, subject, timestamp, and a record of any attachments.

issues.summary: PH: Send Message (With Attachments)
issues.description:
h2. User story

*As a* Recruiter,
*I want to* send an email that includes uploaded attachments and see it appear in-thread,
*So that* I can securely exchange formal documents (like offers or benefits details) with the candidate or agency user directly from Workday.
h2. Scenarios
h3. Scenario 1: Happy path send with attachments (Blobatory Reference)

*Given* I have a valid composed email state that includes one or more visually attached files
*When* I click the "Send" button
*Then* the file references (Blobatory IDs) are successfully included in the message payload dispatched to the backend via REST API
*And* the message, including visual pills/indicators for the attachments, appears in the sliding side panel conversation thread with a "Sent" status tag.
